openmeter/billing/charges/models/chargem.purpose: Provides a reusable Ent mixin (chargemeta.Mixin) injecting all common charge fields (customer_id, service/billing/full-service periods, status, currency, managed_by, subscription references, advance_after, tax config) into every charge entity, plus generic Create[T]/Update[T]/MapFromDB[T] so each charge type shares identical field-mapping logic without duplication.

patterns.name: RecursiveMixin composition
patterns.description: Export Mixin as a type alias type Mixin = entutils.RecursiveMixin[metaMixin]; the inner metaMixin composes AnnotationsMixin + ResourceMixin. Never embed metaMixin directly.

patterns.name: Generic Create/Update/MapFromDB over interface constraints
patterns.description: All three functions are type-parameterized over Creator[T]/Updater[T]/Getter[T]. Pass the Ent-generated builder/updater/getter; the function handles all field mapping uniformly.

patterns.name: UTC normalization on all timestamps
patterns.description: Every time field calls .UTC() before Create/Update; MapFromDB also .UTC() on read-back. Never store or return non-UTC times.

patterns.name: Atomic subscription reference triple
patterns.description: subscription_id, subscription_phase_id, subscription_item_id must be set atomically — all non-nil or all nil. MapFromDB enforces this guard.
patterns.example: if entity.GetSubscriptionID() != nil && entity.GetSubscriptionPhaseID() != nil && entity.GetSubscriptionItemID() != nil { ... }

patterns.name: Partial unique index on unique_reference_id
patterns.description: The uniqueness constraint is a partial index WHERE unique_reference_id IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL. Never add a plain UNIQUE constraint.
patterns.example: index.Fields("namespace", "customer_id", "unique_reference_id").Annotations(entsql.IndexWhere("unique_reference_id IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL")).Unique()

patterns.name: Normalize before validate in Create/Update
patterns.description: Call intent.Normalized() and meta.NormalizeOptionalTimestamp(advanceAfter) at the top of Create/Update before validation so raw input is coerced to canonical form first.

key_file_guides.role: Entire package: Mixin alias, Creator/Updater/Getter interfaces, CreateInput/UpdateInput structs, and Create/Update/MapFromDB generic functions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a field requires updating all three interfaces and all three generic functions; missing any breaks compilation for every charge entity embedding this mixin.

anti_patterns: Defining per-charge-type copies of common fields instead of embedding chargemeta.Mixin
Storing timestamps without .UTC() normalization
Setting only one or two of the three subscription reference fields
Adding a non-partial unique index on unique_reference_id
Calling Validate() before Normalized() — normalization must happen first

decisions.decision: Type-parameterized Create/Update/MapFromDB over interface constraints rather than concrete Ent types
decisions.rationale: Each charge entity generates distinct Ent builder types; generic functions eliminate copy-paste of field-setting logic across all three.

decisions.decision: intent.Normalized() + meta.NormalizeOptionalTimestamp at the top of Create/Update
decisions.rationale: Normalization before validation coerces invalid raw input (e.g. unrounded advance_after) first so validation only sees canonical values.

code_examples.scenario: Embed chargemeta into a new charge entity schema

openmeter/billing/charges/models/creditr.purpose: Models credit realizations — immutable append-only ledger-linked records tracking which credit amounts have been allocated (positive TypeAllocation) or corrected/reversed (non-positive TypeCorrection) against a charge's service period. Provides an Ent mixin, a typed Realizations collection with correction-planning/validation, and lineage annotations for tracing real-credit vs advance origin.

patterns.name: Allocation/Correction duality in CreateInput.Validate()
patterns.description: TypeAllocation requires positive amount and no CorrectsRealizationID. TypeCorrection requires non-positive amount and a non-nil CorrectsRealizationID. Never create a correction without CorrectsRealizationID.
patterns.example: case TypeAllocation: if !i.Amount.IsPositive() { ... }; case TypeCorrection: if i.CorrectsRealizationID == nil { ... }

patterns.name: SortHint for deterministic correction ordering
patterns.description: Realizations in the same batch must have sequential SortHint; CreateCorrectionRequest reverses by (CreatedAt DESC, SortHint DESC) so the most-recently-applied allocations are corrected first.
patterns.example: slices.SortStableFunc(realizations, func(a, b Realization) int { cmp := a.CreatedAt.Compare(b.CreatedAt); if cmp != 0 { return cmp }; return a.SortHint - b.SortHint })

patterns.name: Correction planning via Realizations.Correct
patterns.description: Use Realizations.Correct(amount, currency, cb) — it plans the CorrectionRequest, validates, invokes the callback to produce CreateCorrectionInputs, normalizes, re-validates, and returns CreateInputs. Never bypass this pipeline.
patterns.example: inputs, err := realizations.Correct(amount.Neg(), currency, func(req CorrectionRequest) (CreateCorrectionInputs, error) { return mapRequestToInputs(req) })

patterns.name: Lineage annotations via LineageAnnotations helper
patterns.description: Set lineage origin with LineageAnnotations(originKind) before persisting an allocation; read back with LineageOriginKindFromAnnotations. Never inline the annotation key string.

patterns.name: SelfReferenceType required on Mixin instantiation
patterns.description: creditrealization.Mixin must be instantiated with SelfReferenceType set to the concrete Ent entity pointer to generate the allocation->corrections self-referencing edge; omitting it silently drops the edge.

key_file_guides.role: Core domain types: CreateInput, Type enum, Realization, CreateInputs slice with Validate/Sum. Type invariants (positive allocation, non-positive correction) live here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Relaxing type invariants here lets corrupt state reach persistence.

key_file_guides.role: Realizations collection methods: CreateCorrectionRequest, Correct/CorrectAll, AsCreditsApplied, internal allocationsWithCorrections sort/group.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ErrInsufficientFunds wraps a GenericValidationError; callers must errors.Is-check it explicitly.

key_file_guides.role: CorrectionRequest / CreateCorrectionInput with ValidateWith methods cross-checking existing realizations and totals.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ValidateWith mutates a local copy of realizationsWithRemainingAmountByID to track remaining amounts; logic breaks if the same allocation appears twice incorrectly.

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixin with Creator/Getter interfaces and Create/MapFromDB helpers. Edges method is on the outer Mixin struct, not inner mixinBase.
key_file_guides.watch_for: SelfReferenceType must be set on instantiation; the self-reference edge is added only when non-nil.

key_file_guides.role: LineageOriginKind and LineageSegmentState enums plus annotation read/write helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: LineageOriginKindFromAnnotations silently skips realizations missing the annotation (no error) — intentional for backward compat.

key_file_guides.role: InitialLineageSpecs derives InitialLineageSpec entries from a Realizations slice for seeding lineage; only processes TypeAllocation with a valid lineage annotation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses ulid.Make() for LineageID — IDs are not stable across calls.

anti_patterns: Creating a TypeCorrection without going through Realizations.Correct or validating CorrectsRealizationID and remaining amount
Assigning SortHint=0 to all realizations in a batch — ordering becomes undefined for correction planning
Using amounts without rounding via currencyx.Calculator.RoundToPrecision before creating corrections
Instantiating creditrealization.Mixin without SelfReferenceType when the entity has self-referencing corrections
Reading the AnnotationLineageOriginKind string key directly instead of LineageOriginKindFromAnnotations

decisions.decision: Two-type realization model (allocation + correction) instead of mutable balance rows
decisions.rationale: Immutable append-only records provide a full audit trail; corrections always reference the allocation they reverse so lineage is unambiguous.

decisions.decision: Correction planning in pure Go before DB write
decisions.rationale: Planning in Go allows dry-run validation, amount splitting across allocations, and unit-testing the correction algorithm without a DB.

code_examples.scenario: Plan and apply corrections for a partial refund

openmeter/billing/charges/models/invoice.purpose: Models accrued usage snapshots linking a charge's computed totals to an optional standard invoice line. Provides an Ent mixin (invoicedusage.Mixin) and the AccruedUsage domain type capturing service period, billing totals, and an optional ledger transaction reference.

patterns.name: Mutable flag semantics in AccruedUsage.Validate()
patterns.description: When mutable=false LineID must be non-nil (usage locked to an invoice line); when mutable=true LineID may be nil. Never persist mutable=false with LineID=nil.
patterns.example: if !r.Mutable { if r.LineID == nil { errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("line ID is required when mutable is false")) } }

patterns.name: totals.Mixin composition for billing totals
patterns.description: The Ent mixin embeds totals.Mixin{} to store billing totals alongside usage. Use totals.Set(creator, invoicedUsage.Totals) in Create and totals.FromDB(dbEntity) in MapAccruedUsageFromDB.

patterns.name: Nil-safe ledger transaction reference
patterns.description: LedgerTransaction is optional (*ledgertransaction.GroupReference); extract the group ID pointer safely for SetNillableLedgerTransactionGroupID, and reconstruct it in MapAccruedUsageFromDB only when non-nil.
patterns.example: var trnsGroupID *string; if invoicedUsage.LedgerTransaction != nil { trnsGroupID = &invoicedUsage.LedgerTransaction.TransactionGroupID }

patterns.name: Mixin as type alias (not struct)
patterns.description: type Mixin = entutils.RecursiveMixin[mixin] is a type alias and cannot carry instance fields. Use a standalone struct with embedded RecursiveMixin only when instance fields are needed (see creditrealization.Mixin).

key_file_guides.role: AccruedUsage struct with Validate(); the mutable/LineID invariant and Totals validation live here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A TODO notes LineID will be removed once flat-fee stores line assignment on its own realization state — don't add dependencies on LineID always being present.

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixin + Creator/Getter interfaces + Create/MapAccruedUsageFromDB generic helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Mixin is a type alias and cannot carry instance fields, unlike creditrealization.Mixin which is a struct.

anti_patterns: Persisting AccruedUsage with mutable=false and LineID=nil
Bypassing totals.Set/totals.FromDB and setting totals fields manually on the creator
Adding a non-nil LedgerTransaction with an empty TransactionGroupID

decisions.decision: Separate AccruedUsage entity instead of embedding usage data into the charge entity
decisions.rationale: A charge may accrue usage across multiple billing periods, and each snapshot can independently transition from mutable to immutable as invoice lines are assigned.

openmeter/billing/charges/models/ledgert.purpose: Provides GroupReference and TimedGroupReference value types used across all charge sub-packages to reference ledger transaction groups. GroupReference carries a TransactionGroupID string; TimedGroupReference adds the authorization/settlement timestamp.

patterns.name: Nil-safe pointer helpers for Ent SetNillable* methods
patterns.description: Use GetIDOrNull() for *string and GetTimeOrNull() for *time.Time from a possibly nil *TimedGroupReference. Always use these helpers when passing to Ent SetNillable* methods.

patterns.name: Validate via NewNillableGenericValidationError
patterns.description: Both GroupReference.Validate() and TimedGroupReference.Validate() use models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...)), returning nil when the error slice is empty. Callers should not special-case empty errs.

key_file_guides.role: Complete package: GroupReference and TimedGroupReference structs with Validate() and nil-safe pointer getters.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetIDOrNull()/GetTimeOrNull() on a nil *TimedGroupReference return nil safely — always call on the pointer receiver so nil-deref is safe.

anti_patterns: Accessing TransactionGroupID directly without calling Validate() first
Passing GroupReference by value to SetNillable Ent methods instead of GetIDOrNull()
Constructing TimedGroupReference with a zero Time value — Validate() rejects it

decisions.decision: Separate GroupReference and TimedGroupReference types instead of inline string fields
decisions.rationale: Ledger transaction group IDs appear across multiple charge sub-packages (creditrealization, payment, invoicedusage) with consistent validation needs; a shared type prevents validation drift.

openmeter/billing/charges/models/payment.purpose: Models payment settlements for charges with two concrete variants — External (direct external payment) and Invoiced (settled via a standard invoice line) — sharing a typed Base struct. Provides Ent mixins, domain error sentinels as ValidationIssue values with HTTP status codes, and generic Create/Update/MapFromDB helpers.

patterns.name: Base->Payment->Invoiced/External type hierarchy
patterns.description: Base holds mutable fields; Payment embeds Base + NamespacedID + ManagedModel; Invoiced and External embed Payment with type-specific fields. Create/Update/MapFromDB are layered accordingly.
patterns.example: func CreateInvoiced[T InvoicedCreator[T]](creator InvoicedCreator[T], in InvoicedCreate) T { creator = Create(creator, in.Namespace, in.Base); creator = creator.SetInvoiceID(in.InvoiceID); return creator.SetLineID(in.LineID) }

patterns.name: Status invariants in Base.Validate()
patterns.description: StatusAuthorized requires Authorized != nil; StatusSettled requires both Authorized and Settled != nil. New status values must add a validation case.
patterns.example: case StatusSettled: if r.Settled == nil { errs = append(errs, ...) }; if r.Authorized == nil { errs = append(errs, ...) }

patterns.name: Domain error sentinels as ValidationIssue with HTTP status
patterns.description: Package-level errors (ErrPaymentAlreadyAuthorized, ErrPaymentAlreadySettled, ErrCannotSettleNotAuthorizedPayment) are models.ValidationIssue values with HTTP 400 via commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute. Use these sentinels directly.

patterns.name: InvoicedMixin composes base Mixin + immutable line_id/invoice_id
patterns.description: Use InvoicedMixin (= entutils.RecursiveMixin[invoicedMixin]) for invoiced-payment Ent entities; it composes Mixin{} and adds line_id + invoice_id as Immutable fields.

patterns.name: convert.TimePtrIn(ptr, time.UTC) in Update
patterns.description: In the generic Update function, all timestamp pointers pass through convert.TimePtrIn(ptr, time.UTC) before being set to ensure UTC storage. Never pass a raw pointer without timezone conversion.

key_file_guides.role: Base, Payment, Status enum, attribute key constants. Status invariants and amount-must-be-positive validation live here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PaymentSettlementTypeExternal / PaymentSettlementTypeStandardInvoice are plain string constants (TODO to make typed) — don't add a third as a bare string.

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixin for base payment fields, Creator/Updater/Getter interfaces, generic Create/Update/mapPaymentFromDB.
key_file_guides.watch_for: convert.TimePtrIn(ptr, time.UTC) is required in Update for timestamp fields; raw pointers cause UTC drift.

key_file_guides.role: Invoiced type with line_id + invoice_id, InvoicedCreator interface, InvoicedMixin for the Ent schema.
key_file_guides.watch_for: line_id and invoice_id are Immutable in the Ent schema — they cannot be updated after creation.

key_file_guides.role: External type and ExternalCreateInput; ExternalMixin = Mixin (no extra fields). CreateExternal delegates to the generic Create.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ErrorAttributes() includes the type string PaymentSettlementTypeExternal — keep in sync with models.go constants.

key_file_guides.role: Package-level error sentinels — all ValidationIssue with critical severity and HTTP 400.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Each error has an ErrorCode that is a stable wire identifier — don't rename codes without a compatibility check.

anti_patterns: Creating a StatusSettled payment without Authorized reference set
Mutating line_id or invoice_id on an existing Invoiced entity — they are Immutable
Returning raw fmt.Errorf for domain payment errors instead of the package-level ValidationIssue sentinels
Adding a new payment variant by copying Base fields instead of embedding Base via the mixin hierarchy
Omitting convert.TimePtrIn(ptr, time.UTC) when writing timestamp pointers in the Update path

decisions.decision: Separate Invoiced and External as distinct types sharing a Payment base
decisions.rationale: Invoiced payments settle through the OpenMeter invoice flow and carry immutable line_id + invoice_id; External payments have no invoice counterpart. Keeping them separate prevents incorrect field-presence assumptions.

decisions.decision: ValidationIssue sentinels with HTTP status attributes instead of generic errors
decisions.rationale: The HTTP error encoder reads httpStatusCodeErrorAttribute to produce the correct status; domain errors must carry it to avoid falling through to a 500.
